blocks.content: Wizard's parents were dumbfounded.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's father
blocks.content: What is she saying? You shouldn't talk about dying so carelessly.
The father apologized.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's father
blocks.content: I'm sorry. She's still a child and doesn't understand the ways of the world. She probably doesn't even know what dying means.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I do know. It's really scary.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As a magician of super logic, she would have already felt all the possible outcomes that could happen to her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, it's very scary.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Dying might not be the end. You could break an arm or suffer something terrible. Someone could cut your body with a knife…
The mother was on the verge of tears.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's mother
blocks.content: Stop! Please stop!

blocks.speaker: Wizard's mother
blocks.content: Why are you saying such horrible things? She's only seven years old! Save the world yourself!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I will.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't want to send Wizard. That's why I'm trying to scare her even more. So, are you still not scared?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: No, I'm fine.
Wizard didn't hesitate.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I might get hurt, and it might be painful. But I'll still do it. I want to keep going.
This child really understands.
She had never experienced it, but she understood it through insight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You're a magician.
Wizard, smiling as if she had been praised, turned to her parents and drove the point home.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Mom. I want to follow my brother. Please let me go, okay?
The sudden change in their daughter shocked the mother.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's mother
blocks.content: Wi-Wizard.
Just that morning, she had been acting immaturely, causing headaches for the teachers.
Lampa's eyes narrowed.
She was waiting. No, she might not have thought of it consciously, but deep down…
Was she afraid?
No one could give her the right answer. Loneliness, anxiety, fear. Those negative emotions must have manifested as a sense of superiority.
But Wizard now was different.
All her senses are fiercely absorbing Shirone. What is this, and what must be done to become like this?
It was one of the correct answers.
As the parents, who realized Wizard's determination, were at a loss for words, the administrator intervened.

blocks.speaker: Administrator
blocks.content: Ahem, please come in. Now that we've heard Wizard's determination, let's proceed with the detailed procedures…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's no need for that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We'll give you the amount Stang wants. If there are other conditions, tell Mr. Lampa.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's father
blocks.content: My daughter is not an object! Even if Wizard wants it, we still oppose it!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I also think it's unreasonable. I just confirmed her determination, but my thoughts haven't changed.
Shirone knelt on one knee in front of Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Listen carefully, Wizard. The enemy you will face is one of the strongest humans in the world. No, he might not even be called human.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: So I can't send you right away. You will stay here for a while, and I will test you. It will be very harsh and painful. If you pass, you will become a magician like me. But if you don't pass…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You are not ready to face the world yet. You will wait until I call you again. Can you promise that?
Although he knew Wizard's determination, the opponent was Havitz, known as the extreme villain of the era.
As long as Satan remains, humanity has no future. If everything is going to be ruined anyway, I'd rather do it myself…
It was the only consideration he could give Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Okay. I'll do that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Although it took longer than usual, when the answer finally came, Shirone nodded and turned to the administrator.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nothing is decided yet. We will stay at the magic school for a while and teach Wizard. Of course, if you want, other students can join the classes.

blocks.speaker: Administrator
blocks.content: It was a decision to gain the consent of the Stang Kingdom, and as expected, the administrator swallowed hard.
The stars of the Ivory Tower at the school…
Although it was disappointing not to secure the contract immediately, this much would satisfy the king.

blocks.speaker: Administrator
blocks.content: I will report to His Majesty first. Whether he approves or not will be communicated later.
Ignoring the administrator, Shirone bowed to Wizard's parents.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please entrust Wizard to us. You are welcome to observe the classes anytime. If you still refuse afterward, I won't insist any further.
The couple looked at each other.
They still couldn't understand Shirone's intentions, but they had only one choice.
Kido was strolling through the bustling area where reconstruction was underway.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What am I doing?
He had inadvertently entered human society, but there was no place for him here.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: She's truly evil.
Remembering the humiliation he suffered from Wuorin made him seethe, but the thought of leaving made him regretful.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: There were fun times, too. Maybe I should just give in and go back. I might regret it if I don't.
—Because it's disgusting.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Those words Wuorin spat out stuck in him like a dagger, igniting a fiery impulse.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Ah, forget it! It's so annoying!
He slumped down at the edge of the cobblestone path, glaring at the construction site ahead.
Harvitz was slashing a worker's throat.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Hmm…??

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Maybe my dreams were too big.
With each dance of Harvitz's blade, headless corpses twitched, spurting fountains of blood.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It's not because I'm a goblin, but because Wuorin is an Empress. Even normal humans can't look down on her.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: With that thought, he felt lighter.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: There's no need to feel inferior. From Wuorin's perspective, humans and I are equally insignificant. At least I'm on the stronger side…
Emerging from the construction site, Harvitz indiscriminately slaughtered the passing civilians.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Of course, that doesn't change anything. After all, she thinks of me as an animal.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Kido sighed deeply as a severed arm of a citizen dropped in front of him.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Yeah, I'll just get some air before going back. I'm smart, I should endure it. Why isn't Shirone coming?
With a clear head, Kido stared blankly at the bloody severed arm.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: That was when it hit him.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: …This is strange.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: He didn't even realize he had spoken those words.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Huh? Strange? No, wait, it is really strange. But what's strange?
He looked up to see Harvitz approaching.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: It's been a while, goblin.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: Let's make this fun. Enjoyably fun.
As Harvitz raised his sword, Kido bit into the flesh of his right hand.
Clang! The blade struck the ground.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Ugh! Ugh!
Kido leaped up, gasping for breath as he racked his brain.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What is this…?
Harvitz still wasn't registering in his perception.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: But within the memories infused with the taste of events, a reality different from the present was unfolding.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Ho?
With a force that could pound the ground, Harvitz slowly lifted his upper body.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: You're an interesting one, indeed.
Chomp! Chomp!

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It's getting clearer.
And just as he caught a blurry silhouette, he pushed off the ground and retreated.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It's Harvitz.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It wasn't a perfect form, but there was definitely a monstrous silhouette wielding a sword.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What to do? Should I run? But I don't even know where I am. If he's right beside me…
He acted as he thought.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: …then!
Kido pulled out a spear from his back and wildly slashed around before rolling on the ground.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It might have been a delusion.
But unlike humans, a goblin's strong survival instinct chose life over shame.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Didn't he come closer? Or did he avoid it?
Kido stuck out his tongue, tasting the air.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: …No good. The chances of Harvitz's cells floating around are low, and it's too faint to even be called a flavor.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: He had no choice but to run.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Wait.
Kido flipped backward to land and sucked blood from the wound on his right hand again.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: There. It's still there. Something terrible is watching me from a blurry shadow.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: If he could kill Harvitz here.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: If I could decapitate the man who gave Wuorin, the Empress of Kashan, the greatest humiliation of her life…

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Poo!
Kido gripped the spear with both hands.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Relying on the taste of memories for an attack is too late. By a rough calculation, it takes about twelve steps for him to reach me, between three to four seconds.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: The imaginary second hand moved.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Two seconds. One second.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Ambushing while the opponent was off-guard was undoubtedly a brilliant strategy, but…

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: I can hear everything,
Harvitz had something called 'God's frequency.'

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Kido, who had activated the earth law 'Geomancer,' rotated on his back and turned in all directions.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Now!
The localized gravity twisted, and Harvitz's feet stuck to the ground as if mired in a swamp.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: Hehe, what an amusing skill.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: For the first time in a long while, a dry orgasm of boredom surged in his heart as he repeated mundane murders.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Get caught! Get caught!
Kido endlessly whirled his spear towards an enemy that might or might not be there.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: As long as the Geomancer is active, he can't run far. The moment of killing is when you realize it!
The image of a spinning top made of blade afterimages approached Harvitz at a terrifying speed.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: As he raised his long sword piercingly, calculating the timing, he muttered softly.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Hell.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: A time not in the laws, 0.666 seconds.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Huh?
A black shadow that sprung from the ground enveloped Harvitz, but time did not stop.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What?!

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: As Kido shouted while rotating, a powerful impact hit his side.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Argh!
Kido, who had braced himself with the spear, slid on the ground and checked the impact point where a black robe stood.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: …Good judgment.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: The wandering six o'clock.
Assuming all actions necessary to achieve something had been taken, he obtained the result.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What are you all?
Without answering Kido's question, the wandering six o'clock returned to the position of hell.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: Harvitz circled around those standing in their own time.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: What?
The place at four o'clock was empty.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: Where did one go?
Hell was human, and without needing an answer, God's frequency told him.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: Hmm, Shirone.
As the phenomenon of vanishing disappeared, Kido was finally able to confirm the fallen bodies around him.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: It was a bizarre and horrific scene, but for Kido, there was a task he needed to resolve first.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: I will protect Wuorin.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: His heart's wounds hadn't healed, but he still couldn't give up on her.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Harvitz! Listen carefully. From here, one step…

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: Okay, lead the way.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Huh?
Harvitz tapped his head.

blocks.speaker: Harvitz
blocks.content: You're saying you'll come out to meet me? With the voice of your heart. Yeah, you were decent for a red.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: This woman is really…!
